task: Design and implement the production frontend for this project. Use only these agents in the adversarial convergence loop: Claude, Kimi, Qwen, ZAI. Personas: Kimi = UX Architect (information architecture, navigation flow, interaction design); Qwen = Visual Design Critic (typography, spacing, color, visual hierarchy, accessibility); ZAI = Frontend Engineer (performance, component structure, responsiveness, code quality). Convergence criteria: all adversarials must approve component structure, visual hierarchy, motion behavior, accessibility compliance, and performance characteristics before implementation. If there is aesthetic disagreement, default to premium/confident Nubank + Flighty over playful choices. Technical stack: React functional components/hooks, Tailwind CSS core utilities only, pure CSS animations, SVG icons, CSS custom properties, mobile-first responsive 375->768->1440. Deliverables through convergence: component architecture map, design token system, core layout shell, at least 3 key screens (Dashboard/Home, Detail/Timeline, Action sheet/Form), motion system, dark/light theme switching. Do NOT use generic Material/Bootstrap/shadcn defaults, decorative chrome, spinners, ease/linear timing, or ship any screen without dark mode. Current repo reality: frontend is currently static files under src/frontend/web and served by src/backend/apps/web_server.py. Existing index.html is a large Alpine/Tailwind static page, but this task explicitly requires React functional components/hooks for the production frontend. The implementation must stay within this repo, preserve backend endpoints, and result in a production-grade frontend that web_server can serve. Before implementation, produce the best approved implementation spec and architecture for this repo, including migration path from current static frontend to the new React frontend.
